ISO27001.A.5.18.1.title: Operational Global Administrators count must be between 2 and 4
ISO27001.A.5.18.1.description: Having too few Global Administrators risks loss of access if credentials are unavailable. Having too many increases the attack surface — each GA account is a potential full-tenant compromise. Two to four is the recommended balance for coverage without excessive exposure.
ISO27001.A.5.18.1.rationale: Having too few Global Administrators risks loss of access if credentials are unavailable. Having too many increases the attack surface — each GA account is a potential full-tenant compromise. Two to four is the recommended balance for coverage without excessive exposure.
ISO27001.A.5.18.1.remediation: The Global Administrator directory role is not activated in this tenant. Activate the role by assigning at least one user, then re-run the assessment

ISO27001.A.5.18.2.title: PIM must manage Global Administrator role (no permanent standing assignments)
ISO27001.A.5.18.2.description: Permanently active privileged roles maintain standing admin access with no time limit or justification requirement. JIT access via PIM limits the window of exposure: even if admin credentials are compromised, the attacker can only act during the brief activation window.
ISO27001.A.5.18.2.rationale: Permanently active privileged roles maintain standing admin access with no time limit or justification requirement. JIT access via PIM limits the window of exposure: even if admin credentials are compromised, the attacker can only act during the brief activation window.
ISO27001.A.5.18.2.remediation: Use eligible assignments with time-bound activation

ISO27001.A.5.18.3.description: Access reviews for guest users detect guests who no longer need tenant access. Without reviews, guest accounts accumulate over time and may retain access long after the collaboration project or relationship that required it has ended.

ISO27001.A.5.18.4.rationale: Access reviews for privileged roles detect and remove stale, unnecessary, or unauthorized role assignments. Without periodic reviews, accumulation of privileged role assignments creates lateral movement opportunities and violates least-privilege principles.

ISO27001.A.5.18.7.title: All Global Administrator accounts must be cloud-only
ISO27001.A.5.18.7.description: Global Administrators control every aspect of the tenant. Standard MFA can be bypassed via adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing attacks that relay the authentication session. Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, Windows Hello, certificate) eliminates this attack class for the highest-privilege accounts.
ISO27001.A.5.18.7.rationale: Global Administrators control every aspect of the tenant. Standard MFA can be bypassed via adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing attacks that relay the authentication session. Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, Windows Hello, certificate) eliminates this attack class for the highest-privilege accounts.
ISO27001.A.5.18.7.remediation: Enroll all Global Administrators in phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business, or certificate-based)

ISO27001.A.5.18.8.title: Global Administrators must not be assigned full M365 E3/E5 productivity licenses
ISO27001.A.5.18.8.description: Admin accounts with full E3/E5 licenses have access to productivity services (Exchange, Teams, SharePoint) which increase their attack surface — more services to phish, more sessions to steal. A minimal license reduces the services accessible via a compromised admin credential.
ISO27001.A.5.18.8.rationale: Admin accounts with full E3/E5 licenses have access to productivity services (Exchange, Teams, SharePoint) which increase their attack surface — more services to phish, more sessions to steal. A minimal license reduces the services accessible via a compromised admin credential.
ISO27001.A.5.18.8.remediation: Assign admin accounts minimal licenses (Entra ID P2). Do not assign E3/E5 productivity suites
ISO27001.A.5.18.8.portal_path: M365 admin center > Users > Active users > Licenses

ISO27001.A.8.3.1.description: Allowing non-admin users to create new Entra tenants enables them to establish separate identity environments outside organizational governance. Rogue tenants can be used to exfiltrate data or establish shadow IT identity providers.
ISO27001.A.8.3.1.rationale: Allowing non-admin users to create new Entra tenants enables them to establish separate identity environments outside organizational governance. Rogue tenants can be used to exfiltrate data or establish shadow IT identity providers.

ISO27001.A.8.5.1.title: All Global Administrators must have MFA registered
ISO27001.A.8.5.1.description: Applications with expired credentials remain registered in the tenant but may still be trusted by services that have not rotated their token cache. Expired credential remnants also indicate that application lifecycle management processes are not operating correctly.
ISO27001.A.8.5.1.rationale: Applications with expired credentials remain registered in the tenant but may still be trusted by services that have not rotated their token cache. Expired credential remnants also indicate that application lifecycle management processes are not operating correctly.

ISO27001.A.5.15.1.description: Security Defaults enforce a baseline of MFA for all users and block legacy authentication — but they are incompatible with Conditional Access and are typically disabled when CA policies are in place. A tenant with Security Defaults disabled and insufficient CA policies has no enforced authentication baseline.
ISO27001.A.5.15.1.rationale: Security Defaults enforce a baseline of MFA for all users and block legacy authentication — but they are incompatible with Conditional Access and are typically disabled when CA policies are in place. ISO27001.A.8.5.2.title: Telephony-based authentication methods (SMS and Voice) must be disabled
ISO27001.A.8.5.2.description: Per-user MFA is a legacy enforcement method that applies MFA independently of Conditional Access. It does not support modern features like risk-based enforcement, device trust, or named location exceptions. Running both per-user MFA and CA simultaneously creates conflicts and gaps.
ISO27001.A.8.5.2.rationale: Per-user MFA is a legacy enforcement method that applies MFA independently of Conditional Access. It does not support modern features like risk-based enforcement, device trust, or named location exceptions. Running both per-user MFA and CA simultaneously creates conflicts and gaps.
ISO27001.A.8.5.2.remediation: Use Conditional Access policies for MFA enforcement instead of per-user MFA

ISO27001.A.8.5.3.title: Email OTP authentication must be disabled
ISO27001.A.8.5.3.description: Email OTP authentication sends a one-time code to an email address, which may itself not be MFA-protected. If the email account is compromised, the email OTP provides no additional security — it's a second factor that is only as strong as the first.
ISO27001.A.8.5.3.rationale: Email OTP authentication sends a one-time code to an email address, which may itself not be MFA-protected. If the email account is compromised, the email OTP provides no additional security — it's a second factor that is only as strong as the first.
ISO27001.A.8.5.3.remediation: Email OTP is a weaker authentication factor

ISO27001.A.8.5.4.title: Custom banned password lists must be enabled
ISO27001.A.8.5.4.description: LAPS randomizes the local administrator password on each managed device, preventing credential reuse attacks. ISO27001.A.8.5.5.title: Smart Lockout threshold must be 10 or fewer attempts
ISO27001.A.8.5.5.description: Mailboxes hidden from the Global Address List (GAL) cannot be found through normal directory lookup, which may indicate misconfiguration, legacy service accounts with active mailboxes, or deliberate concealment. All active mailboxes should be visible and accounted for in the directory.
ISO27001.A.8.5.5.rationale: Mailboxes hidden from the Global Address List (GAL) cannot be found through normal directory lookup, which may indicate misconfiguration, legacy service accounts with active mailboxes, or deliberate concealment. All active mailboxes should be visible and accounted for in the directory.
ISO27001.A.8.5.5.remediation: Investigate hidden user mailboxes. Mailboxes hidden from the Global Address List may indicate a compromised account. Review: Get-Mailbox -Filter "HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled -eq $true -and RecipientTypeDetails -eq UserMailbox"
